selectedShortStories.title: The Lottery
selectedShortStories.author: Shirley Jackson
selectedShortStories.description: A village holds an annual lottery where the 'winner' is stoned to death by neighbors

selectedShortStories.title: The Gift of the Magi
selectedShortStories.author: O. Henry
selectedShortStories.description: Poor couple sell their treasures to buy each other Christmas gifts that become useless

selectedShortStories.title: The Metamorphosis
selectedShortStories.author: Franz Kafka
selectedShortStories.description: Man wakes up as giant bug, family gradually rejects him until he dies

selectedShortStories.title: The Necklace
selectedShortStories.author: Guy de Maupassant
selectedShortStories.description: Woman borrows 'expensive' necklace, loses it, works 10 years to replace it, learns it was fake

selectedShortStories.title: A Rose for Emily
selectedShortStories.author: William Faulkner
selectedShortStories.description: Town discovers reclusive woman has been sleeping next to her dead lover's corpse for years

selectedShortStories.title: Hills Like White Elephants
selectedShortStories.author: Ernest Hemingway
selectedShortStories.description: Couple discusses abortion without ever saying the word

selectedShortStories.title: The Dead
selectedShortStories.author: James Joyce
selectedShortStories.description: Man realizes his wife still loves someone who died for her years ago

selectedShortStories.title: Cathedral
selectedShortStories.author: Raymond Carver
selectedShortStories.description: Prejudiced man learns empathy by drawing a cathedral with blind visitor

selectedShortStories.title: The Lady with the Dog
selectedShortStories.author: Anton Chekhov
selectedShortStories.description: Vacation affair becomes unexpected true love for two married people

selectedShortStories.title: A Good Man Is Hard to Find
selectedShortStories.author: Flannery O'Connor
selectedShortStories.description: Family meets escaped killer on vacation; grandmother's selfishness dooms them all

selectedShortStories.title: The Story of an Hour
selectedShortStories.author: Kate Chopin
selectedShortStories.description: Woman feels free when told husband died, then dies of shock when he walks in alive

selectedShortStories.title: Flowers for Algernon
selectedShortStories.author: Daniel Keyes
selectedShortStories.description: Disabled man becomes genius through experiment, then regresses back

selectedShortStories.title: Harrison Bergeron
selectedShortStories.author: Kurt Vonnegut
selectedShortStories.description: Future where government forces everyone to be equal through handicaps

selectedShortStories.title: The Ones Who Walk Away from Omelas
selectedShortStories.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
selectedShortStories.description: Perfect city's happiness depends on one child's endless suffering

selectedShortStories.title: The Birds
selectedShortStories.author: Daphne du Maurier
selectedShortStories.description: Birds suddenly start attacking humans everywhere

selectedShortStories.title: The Veldt
selectedShortStories.author: Ray Bradbury
selectedShortStories.description: Virtual reality nursery becomes too real, children use it to kill parents

selectedShortStories.title: The Scarlet Ibis
selectedShortStories.author: James Hurst
selectedShortStories.description: Boy pushes disabled brother too hard to be 'normal,' brother dies

selectedShortStories.title: Désirée's Baby
selectedShortStories.author: Kate Chopin
selectedShortStories.description: Husband rejects wife thinking she has Black ancestry, turns out it's him

selectedShortStories.title: The Bet
selectedShortStories.author: Anton Chekhov
selectedShortStories.description: Lawyer bets 15 years in prison to prove life imprisonment better than death penalty

selectedShortStories.title: The Lady, or the Tiger?
selectedShortStories.author: Frank R. Stockton
selectedShortStories.description: Princess must choose whether lover opens door to marriage or death

metadata.notes: These stories were selected for their literary impact, emotional power, and suitability for BookBridge's simplified reading approach. Public domain stories can be implemented immediately.
